question_text: A light ray travelling through glass hits the glass-air boundary at an angle of incidence of 50°. The critical angle for this glass-air boundary is 42°. What happens to the light ray?
correct_answer: The light undergoes total internal reflection and does not escape into the air; it is completely reflected back into the glass.
explanation: When the angle of incidence (50°) exceeds the critical angle (42°), total internal reflection occurs. All light is reflected back into the denser medium (glass), and none refracts into the air. This is used in optical fibres and prisms.
